title: Author PROP-014 amendment-001 — answer 6 operator clarifying questions before authorizing implementation
summary: Operator reviewed PROP-014. Six clarifying questions before authorizing implementation. Tinker authors monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-014-amendment-001.json with explicit answers to each. Two of the questions (C16/C17) flagged in PROP-014 itself as latent risks; the other four came up during operator review of the proposal as a whole. Implementation path is decided (Path C — hybrid: operator implements Mech 2/1b/3, tinker authors verify-pending-state.js + workspace-sync integration as a separate follow-up directive after the amendment lands), but answers to these questions may shift the design and therefore the implementation scope.

context.trigger: Operator review of PROP-014 (commit pending workspace-sync rescue at time of writing) on 2026-05-02 morning. PROP is well-structured and tinker accepted operator's first-cut taxonomy with one valuable refinement (Class 1 split into 1a/1b sub-mechanisms). Forensic trace verified against git, sense-check audit identified 18 cross-agent couplings of which 16 are safe-or-covered and 2 are latent. Implementation deferred until clarifications land.
context.relationship_to_prop_014: This directive does NOT supersede PROP-014. PROP-014 status remains 'proposed.' Tinker writes a sibling amendment file. Operator may then either (a) authorize PROP-014 as-amended for implementation, or (b) request further iterations. Amendment is additive — it does not mutate PROP-014's existing fields, only adds clarification. PROP-014 may optionally have an amendment_history field appended pointing to the amendment file (additive edit, same family as the PROP-009 popped_by_queue_id exception).

deliverable_requested.schema: Single JSON file. Top-level: id, parent_prop_id, created_at, authored_by, directive_id, questions (array of 6 question/answer objects), summary_of_changes, recommended_followup. Each question object: question_id, question_summary, operator_framing, tinker_answer, design_impact, files_to_modify_in_prop_014_if_authorized.

questions.question_summary: C16 latent risk — curmudgeon alerts.txt based on priority-queue. Why not covered by Mech 2? Mech 2 extension or defer?
questions.operator_framing: PROP-014 sense_check_broader_audit classifies C16 as 'latent-risk-not-fully-covered' but does not propose handling. Tinker says Mech 2 partially covers it. Operator wants explicit decision: (a) extend Mech 2 to cover the curmudgeon-write-side (alerts.txt depends on priority-queue state which is multi-writer); (b) defer as known-tolerable risk; (c) propose a small Mech 4 specifically for curmudgeon's alert-authoring path. If (a), what's the diff sketch? If (b), what's the disprove condition that would force a future revisit? If (c), spec the Mech 4.
questions.answer_shape: Pick one of (a/b/c), justify, and provide the additional diff sketch / disprove condition / spec as appropriate.

questions.question_summary: C17 latent risk — analyst-mode1b writes verdict on predictions.json that poller reads for window-closure detection. Why not covered? Mech 1b extension or defer?
questions.operator_framing: Same family as Q1. Mode 1b writes verdicts that drive poller's downstream behavior. PROP-014's Mech 1b focuses on Mode 5 + extension to Modes 0/1b/2b. Question: does the existing Mech 1b spec (in proposed_fix.mechanism_1_write_verify.sub_mechanism_1b_write_verify_analyst) actually cover the Mode 1b verdict-write path, or is that a separate latent gap? If it covers, point to the specific bullet. If it doesn't, propose an extension or defer with rationale.
questions.answer_shape: Either (i) cite the specific Mech 1b bullet that covers Mode 1b verdict-writes, OR (ii) propose extension diff sketch, OR (iii) defer with disprove condition.

questions.question_summary: Mech 3 measurement — the disprove condition has two clauses ('grep against confabulation patterns' AND 'cannot be traced to JSON fields/log lines'). First clause is automatable. Is the second clause automatable, or is Mech 3 inherently dependent on manual operator review?
questions.operator_framing: Mech 3 (narrative-cite discipline) ships last per sequencing. Operator concern: if the post-fix measurement is mostly manual sampling, Mech 3 is more aspiration than enforcement. Tinker's measurement_plan.disprove_conditions.mechanism_3_failed_if includes the trace-to-source clause but doesn't say how to evaluate it. Specifically: can a script automatically check that every state-bearing claim in daily-report prose has a corresponding citation to a JSON field or log line from the same run? If yes, sketch the script's logic. If no, what's the operator's manual review cadence and effort estimate?
questions.answer_shape: Either (i) propose script logic + acceptance threshold, OR (ii) acknowledge manual-only and specify cadence/effort. If (ii), is Mech 3 worth shipping at all given lower enforceability? Tinker's recommendation.

questions.question_summary: Mid-rollout parallelize permission — under what trigger conditions can operator pull remaining mechanisms forward into parallel implementation rather than waiting through sequential rollout?
questions.operator_framing: Sequencing argues for sequential implementation because diagnostic value > speed. But the operator-pain argument grows during rollout if bugs persist. Operator wants to formalize the escalation criterion now, not mid-fire. Specifically: (a) what observation during rollout would justify parallelizing remaining mechanisms? (b) does parallelizing forfeit the per-mechanism diagnostic, or only blur it? (c) is there a middle option (e.g., 'parallelize only the mechanism whose fix is uncontroversial, keep diagnostic on the others')?
questions.answer_shape: Define explicit trigger conditions for mid-rollout parallelize. For each trigger, name which mechanisms can be pulled forward and what diagnostic loss results.

questions.question_summary: Mech 2 scope — enumerate exact read-sites that get the verify-on-read discipline. PROP narrows it to 'critical declaration paths' but is that decider-creating-new-issues only, or also re-declaring existing ones, or also curmudgeon chaining cycles?
questions.operator_framing: PROP-014's Mech 2 says 'verify-on-read for daily-report critical declarations.' Operator wants exhaustive enumeration: every cross-agent read-site where Mech 2's discipline applies. Examples we want classified: (a) decider creating ISS-NNN with severity:critical based on a curmudgeon review, (b) decider re-flagging an existing ISS as critical based on a stale curmudgeon review, (c) curmudgeon's chained-cycle reads of prior reviews when authoring c2/c3/cN, (d) decider's daily-report `pipeline_status.curmudgeon` narration that summarizes curmudgeon state, (e) analyst Mode 2b reading attention-inbox to dispatch work. For each: in-scope or out-of-scope for Mech 2, with reasoning.
questions.answer_shape: Exhaustive table. Each row: read-site, in/out scope, reasoning. If out-of-scope but at risk of similar bug class, flag for follow-up Mech.

questions.question_summary: CLAUDE.md additive-edit exception for operator-directives. Tinker just wrote completed_at + completed_by_run + prop_id_authored to DIRECTIVE-20260502-001. That's an additive edit on an append-only file. Should be formalized like the PROP-009 exception for curmudgeon reviews.
questions.operator_framing: CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules section already has an explicit 'PROP-009 additive-edit exception' for the decider writing popped_by_queue_id onto curmudgeon review files. Same pattern needed here: tinker writes specific lifecycle fields to operator-directives at completion time. Should be formalized so the rule is deliberate. Propose exact wording for a new additive-edit exception block in CLAUDE.md.
questions.answer_shape: Exact replacement text for CLAUDE.md, suitable for direct insertion. Specify which fields tinker may write (completed_at, completed_by_run, prop_id_authored, status:completed, plus any others needed for partial-progress + supersede flows). Each field's idempotency rule. Each field's read-side guard (other agents must not parse these as primary state).

deliverable_requested.do_not_implement: DO NOT make any prompt edits, schema changes, or file writes outside of the amendment file itself + optional additive append to PROP-014's amendment_history field. Operator reviews and authorizes implementation in a separate step. Treat this as a one-shot amendment-authorship task.

non_goals: Do NOT re-author PROP-014 itself (mutation of existing fields). Amendment is sibling additive document.
Do NOT propose new mechanisms beyond what answering the questions requires. Stay focused on the 6 questions.
Do NOT expand to new bug instances or new latent risks beyond C16/C17 — those will come from observing post-fix state.
Do NOT implement the Path C plan (operator implements Mech 2/1b/3, tinker authors verifier script in separate followup directive). Path C is for AFTER the amendment lands.

proposed_fix_spec.note_to_tinker: Each of the 6 questions has an operator_framing and answer_shape. Stay within the answer_shape. If you find a question that needs more nuance than the answer_shape allows, expand it AND note that you expanded it (don't silently ignore the answer_shape constraint).

completion_criteria.answer_quality: Each answer is concrete enough that operator can read it and authorize/reject without further follow-up. Vague answers (e.g., 'depends on context') without follow-up specifics are not acceptable.
completion_criteria.self_consistency: Answers do not contradict the existing PROP-014. If an answer would require revising PROP-014, flag the revision explicitly in design_impact field rather than silently revising.
completion_criteria.cross_reference: Each answer cites the specific PROP-014 section it relates to (e.g., 'PROP-014 sense_check_broader_audit.couplings.C16').
completion_criteria.mark_directive_completed: Set this directive's status to 'completed' with completed_at + completed_by_run + amendment_file_authored fields appended. Do not mutate other fields.

cross_references: CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules section, PROP-009 additive-edit exception (template for Q6)
